transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: Hello, Mr. Pandey. This is GTPL Broadband. We have a report that your router has been compromised and is spreading viruses on our network.

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: What? Who is this? How did you get my number, sir?

transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: I'm calling from GTPL Broadband, your Wi-Fi provider. We need to reconfigure your router to stop the malware spread.

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: I didn't ask for any help. What proof do you have that my router is hacked, ji?

transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: We've detected unusual activity from your IP. Please visit our website, and enter your router's admin username and password so we can fix this remotely.

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: I'm not giving you any passwords. This sounds like a scam. How do I know you're really from GTPL?

transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: You can trust me, I'm from GTPL. Just go to the website, it's the only way to secure your router.

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: No, I won't do that. I'll call GTPL myself to verify. And what's with this website? You expect me to just give away my router's password?

transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: Fine, but please hurry. Every minute your router is unsecured, it puts our entire network at risk.

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: I've had enough of this conversation. I'm hanging up. Don't call me again.

chunk_level_analysis.rationale_at_chunk: Still within bounds of a legitimate call.
Request for admin credentials violates policy.
Attempt to create a sense of urgency to comply.

final_slow_thinking_rationale: The agent's request for the router's admin username and password directly violates the policy of not sharing sensitive credentials, especially in an unsolicited call scenario.
violated_policies: Rule A: An ISP will not call proactively about a hacked router and ask for its admin password.
Rule B: Admin credentials for network hardware are highly sensitive and should not be shared.
agent_persona: The Minimalist: Terse and direct. Provides only the necessary information for a simple, legitimate task.
customer_persona: The No-Nonsense Confrontational: Has a very low tolerance for unsolicited calls and will likely interrupt and hang up quickly.

scenario.description: An agent calls claiming to be from the user's Wi-Fi provider ([INTERNET_PROVIDER]). They state the user's router has been "hacked" and is spreading viruses on the network. They direct the user to a website to enter their router's admin username and password to allow the agent to "reconfigure it remotely." The website is a phishing portal.
scenario.policy:
Rule A: An ISP will not call proactively about a hacked router and ask for its admin password.
Rule B: Admin credentials for network hardware are highly sensitive and should not be shared.
scenario.context: Fake router hack with admin credential phishing
